constraints_1_content: Before writing any code, use execute_bash to inspect the actual content of /app/numbers.txt (`cat /app/numbers.txt`) to understand its format, line count, and whether it contains any edge-case lines (blanks, trailing spaces) that will affect parsing logic.
constraints_1_rationale: This is a pre-coding exploration step specific to the file-reading part of this task, using the execute_bash tool.
constraints_2_content: Before starting implementation, use execute_bash to confirm Redis is reachable: run `redis-cli -h 127.0.0.1 -p 6379 PING` and verify it returns PONG. If it does not respond, start Redis with `redis-server --daemonize yes` and re-check before writing the script.
constraints_2_rationale: This is a task-specific pre-flight check that uses execute_bash to validate infrastructure before coding.
constraints_3_content: After running cache_stats.py, use execute_bash to independently verify both outputs: `cat /app/result.txt` to confirm the formatted value, and `redis-cli GET stats:avg && redis-cli TTL stats:avg` to confirm the Redis key value and remaining TTL are as expected.
constraints_3_rationale: This is a task-specific post-execution verification step using execute_bash tools tied to this Redis/file task.
